rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PASCASIO SAINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Payment Of Fees To Court-Appointed Counsel; For Authority To Place Lien On Real Propertyl And For Discharge As Court-Appointed Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests $2,647.50 in fees, which includes $990 for the preparation of this petition, and up to $1,500 anticipated fees. 2) Petitioner requests authority to place a lien against Conservatee's interest in his real property for all fees incurred. This conservatorship is only over the person, and Conservatee's assets are not under court supervision. In addition, previous fees were awarded while attorney was at another law firm.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF INEZ ERTASSI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/20. The proposed distribution must be only to the trustee of the devisee trust, not to the trust beneficiaries. Paragraph 6 of the second amended prayer should show exactly the net assets as shown in paragraph 31 of the second amended petition. If the statutory compensation is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of Receipts and Gains and Losses on Sales must be included. No schedule of Receipts was attached to the second amended petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALVIN KENNER FOOTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/20. No proof of service of the amendment has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] The amendment sets forth the incorrect amount of interest on the pecuniary bequests that started accruing interest after 11/6/19; no calculation is shown. The attorney costs still do not indicate what was E-filed for each of the E-filing charges. The initial filing fee was $450.00; the filing fee for this petition was $435.00. Notice of the second amendment must be given to all interested parties after it is filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 1/14/20. A copy of the Examiner's Notes is not necessary.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/20. Attachment A to the Second Supplement filed on 12/5/19 does not indicate the parties' relationships to the decedent. [LRSF 14.26.] Notice must be mailed directly to a party entitled to notice, not in care of another, unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed in writing that notice be mailed to that other person. [CRC 7.51(a).] Notice was not given to all of the parties listed on Attachment A to the Second Supplement. The proof of service filed on 12/23/19 shows that notice was mailed to just two attorneys and three consular officials on 12/23/19, which was not at least 15 days before this 12/31/19 hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN F. CHRISTENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/15/20. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 A., and 14.93 G.] The petition caption does not disclose that the petitioner is seeking full IAEA authority. Not all persons mentioned in the will appear to be listed in item 8 of the petition. If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, item 8 of the petition must clearly identify the trust beneficiaries, and all heirs and trust beneficiaries must receive notice. Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/20 in order to avoid another automatic continuance.
